content.full_text:
September 11, 1990

Scott Hallerin
Acting General Counsel
Suite 800
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Hallerin,

This is a request for formal written advice on the use of campaign funds by a local ballot committee. The facts are:

The committee (Voters Initiative Committee, called VIC) was formed to get an initiative qualified for the city of Morro Bay election November 6th. It did qualify and is now on the ballot.

The committee continues to function in its role of getting people to vote for the initiative. In that role it is raising money, printing literature etc. The ballot issue, stated simply, is to limit the Williams Bros proposed shopping center to 13 net acres instead of the 30 acre size approved in a prior initiative.

This is probably the most heavily contested issue in the upcoming election. There are two candidates for mayor, one opposing and one endorsing the measure. Of the City Council Candidates, two endorse the measure and two oppose and two views are unknown. Our questions are:

Can any of the funds of the ballot committee be used to:

1. Make a contribution to Rose Marie Sheets, the incumbent mayor, who is not a member of VIC but is running for reelection and supports the initiative?

2. Make a contribution to Susan Mullen, City Council candidate, who is a member of the VIC committee but holds no office in it.

3. Make a contribution to Ben Luna also running for City Council who is the chair of VIC and does have significant influence on the committee?

4. Print a slate in its literature showing which candidates it endorses?

5. Print a list of all candidates and show how they stand on this one measure?

6. Put up yard signs with slates or candidate endorsements?

I realize it may take 21 working days to get your answer but hope we will be able to hear sooner as the election is fast approaching and many of the people in VIC have never been involved in elections and do not understand the intricacies of FPPC rules, and will be awaiting your guidance.

Sincerely,

Joan Mihay
Treasurer
Ben Luna for Council Committee
785 Cabrillo Place
Morro Bay, CA 93442
(805) 772-5225
